title: First and Emergency Aid Intervention Laboratory
content:
First and Emergency Aid Intervention Laboratory

Program

In emergency and critical illness situations, it is very important to be able to make quick and correct decisions, to be careful and cold-blooded, to determine the condition of the patient / injured person with appropriate communication skills and to apply the correct medical intervention. In order to do all these, well-trained "PARAMEDIC" employees are needed.

The aim of the First and Emergency Aid program is to train qualified technicians who are physically healthy and strong, who can make quick and correct decisions, who are careful, cold-blooded and have a strong sense of responsibility, who have high communication skills, who examine the scene and the patient / injured and apply emergency treatment techniques according to their condition, and who can also drive an ambulance.

Purpose of First and Emergency Aid Intervention Laboratory

In line with the above-mentioned objectives, in the First and Emergency Aid Intervention Laboratory, students are taught subjects such as ensuring patient safety to be applied in emergency situations, control of vital signs, parenteral intervention, cardio pulmonary resuscitation, transporting the patient and injured with appropriate methods, use of intervention equipment, case studies are carried out.

In the laboratory, first aid courses taught in other programs are also held, Basic Life Support, Coma Positioning, Shock Positioning, patient / injured transport methods are taught using materials such as models and stretchers.

First and Emergency Aid Response Laboratory Equipment

Portable Aspirator

Defibrillator

Full Size Child CPR Training Manikin

Full Size Baby CPR Training Manikin

IV Arm Injection Training Manikin

Foldable Ambulance Stretcher

Bedside Monitor

Laringascope Sets

Intubation Model

Spine Board

Combination Stretcher

Spoon Stretcher

Blood Pressure Monitors and Stethoscopes

In addition to the mentioned fixtures, there are consumables.

Qualifications Required for First and Emergency Aid Response Technicians

Has basic and clinical medical knowledge about pre-hospital emergency care and rescue.

Has the skills and knowledge to prepare the ambulance for emergency call and patient transportation.

Evaluates the scene and applies the knowledge, skills and behaviors required by pre-hospital emergency patient care.

Applies the professional skills required for preliminary diagnosis and pre-hospital emergency treatment.

Performs emergency medical care and interventions.

Communicates effectively with colleagues, patients, relatives, physicians, nurses and other health professionals.

Takes part in the team to solve complex and unforeseen problems encountered in applications related to the field.

Acts in accordance with the laws, regulations and legislation related to the duties, rights and responsibilities as an individual working in the field of First and Emergency Aid.

Acts in accordance with social, scientific, cultural and ethical values in the stages of collecting, interpreting, applying and announcing the results of data related to the field of First and Emergency Aid.
